REFERENCE: The citation describes a real and well-known paper (Krause et al. 2010, Nature, on the Denisova hominin mitochondrial genome). The paper exists and is correctly identified by title, journal, year, volume, issue, pages, DOI, and PMID. REFERENCE: This is the highly cited and well-known 2010 paper in Nature by Krause et al. describing the complete mitochondrial DNA genome extracted from a finger bone found in Denisova Cave, southern Siberia. The paper revealed an unknown hominin group (later called Denisovans) that was distinct from both modern humans and Neanderthals. All bibliographic details (authors, title, journal, volume, issue, pages, DOI, and PMID) are accurate. The PMCID is in the correct format but its specific assignment requires further verification.
authors: The author list matches the known authors of the famous paper describing the mitochondrial genome of the Denisovan hominin. Krause, Fu, Good, Viola, Shunkov, Derevianko, and Pääbo are all confirmed authors.
title: The title matches the actual title of the 2010 paper published in Nature that reported the Denisovan mitochondrial genome. This is a landmark paper in paleoanthropology.
journal_name: Nature is the correct journal where this paper was published.
journal_volume: Volume 464 is the correct volume for this paper in Nature (2010).
journal_issue: Issue 7290 is correct for this paper.
journal_page: Pages 894-897 match the actual page numbers for this paper in Nature.
elocator: No elocator is applicable for this print-style citation; pages are provided instead.
publication_date: April 8, 2010 matches the actual publication date of this paper.
doi: DOI 10.1038/nature08976 is the correct DOI for this paper. Nature DOI prefixes (10.1038) are accurate.
pmcid: PMCID PMC10152974 appears to be a valid PMC identifier format. However, the original paper from 2010 may have been deposited later in PMC. The PMCID format looks plausible but I cannot fully verify this specific identifier without checking. Note that older Nature papers from 2010 may have been added to PMC retroactively with PMCID numbers that don't match the original deposition date.
pmid: PMID 20336068 is the correct PubMed identifier for this paper.
